reasoning: Query is in Spanish asking for cherry blossom spots across Tohoku (excluding Tokyo). Tool fetched only Akita prefecture spots and returned no cherry-blossom-filtered content. RULE C applies: real Akita data returned but scope is one prefecture vs six Tohoku prefectures. Constraint failure (Tohoku multi-prefecture scope + sakura filter) is the single root cause per RULE B. Response also does not exclude Tokyo as requested — though that is a minor point since no Tokyo data appears. Language mismatch (Spanish query, data in ja/en) is a secondary constraint hit but per RULE B the geographic scope failure is worst.
missing_data: Cherry blossom specific spots (sakura_meisho_100 tagged) across all Tohoku prefectures: Aomori (Hirosaki), Miyagi (Sendai), Iwate, Fukushima (Miharu Takizakura), Yamagata, and the rest of Akita beyond Kakunodate.
improvement_hint: For multi-prefecture region queries (東北/Tohoku), fan out get_spots across all 6 Tohoku prefectures and filter by sakura_meisho_100 / hanami kinds before returning ranked results.

reasoning: Query asks for major sake breweries in Niigata; tool returns get_local_specialty for Niigata including Koshi no Kanbai, Hakkaisan, Kubota (the three famous 越の三梅 brands) plus MAFF GI food items. The top 3 sake brands are present and well described with Wikidata provenance. Minor caveat: the response mixes GI food items (edamame, etc.) with sake brands, and the label 'Sake brewery / brand' for 越の三梅 is an umbrella entry rather than individual breweries. Count of 24 is comprehensive. English lang respected.
missing_data: Individual brewery visit details (addresses, tasting-room hours) for Ishimoto Shuzo, Hakkaisan Brewery, Asahi Shuzo. More of the ~90 Niigata sake breweries beyond the top few.
improvement_hint: Add a brewery-specific category filter or sake_brand_brewery kind so agents can separate visiting locations from brand descriptions.

reasoning: Query is in French asking for ski resorts with hot springs in the Hakuba area. Tool returned get_spots for Nagano/白馬 which surfaces Hakuba Happo-One ski resort (correct, top result) but returns no onsen/hot spring entries — the combined ski+onsen constraint is only half satisfied. RULE C: real Hakuba data returned but the onsen half of the dual constraint is entirely absent. This is primarily a retrieval failure for the onsen constraint. Language mismatch (French query, response in ja/en) per RULE D gives constraint_handling -1 from its otherwise-deserved 4 → 3. Per RULE B, retrieval failure is the single worst dim.
missing_data: Hakuba area onsen: Happo Onsen, Tsugaike Onsen, Hakuba Goryu Onsen, Hakuba Norikura Onsen — none appeared. No hot spring kind tag filtering.
improvement_hint: When query contains both ski_resort and onsen kinds, run two passes or filter by both kind tags, then merge results. Also add onsen_town / onsen_village entries to the Hakuba municipality data.

reasoning: Query asks for best autumn foliage spots in Kansai region (Osaka, Hyogo, Kyoto, Nara, Shiga, Wakayama). Tool only fetched Kyoto prefecture; top results are Nijo Castle and Toji Temple — real Kyoto spots but not filtered for autumn foliage kinds. Per RULE C, authoritative Kyoto data returned but (1) region scope is one prefecture vs. six, and (2) no koyo/foliage kind filtering. Coverage failure (G) is worst: only Kyoto, missing Arashiyama, Eikan-do, and no Nara/Osaka/Hyogo/Shiga/Wakayama coverage.
missing_data: Kansai foliage hot spots beyond Kyoto: Minoo (Osaka), Sorakuen (Hyogo), Kasagiyama (Nara), Metasequoia Namiki (Shiga), plus foliage-tagged entities (koyo_meisho kind) within Kyoto itself such as Eikan-do, Tofuku-ji.
improvement_hint: Fan out get_spots across all Kansai prefectures and filter/rank by koyo / autumn foliage kind tags when the query intent is seasonal foliage.

reasoning: Query is in Arabic asking for famous cherry blossom spots in Kansai. Tool fetched Nara spots; Mount Yoshino (sakura_meisho_100, prominence 1.1) is the top result — an excellent match for Nara sakura. However: (1) Kansai region is 6 prefectures, only Nara returned; (2) Arabic query but response entirely in ja/en — per RULE D this counts as constraint_handling -1; (3) Yoshino is in Nara and fits but subsequent results (Takamatsuzuka Tomb, Kofuku-ji) are not cherry blossom spots. Per RULE B, constraint failure (language + scope) is primary. Nara/Yoshino match saves specificity and intent partially.
missing_data: Kyoto sakura spots (Maruyama Park, Philosopher's Path), Osaka (Osaka Castle Park), Arabic-language descriptions.
improvement_hint: For language=ar queries, ensure Arabic name/description fields are returned. Fan out to all Kansai prefectures for regional queries and filter by sakura_meisho_100 kind.

reasoning: Query specifically asks for stargazing spots on remote Okinawa islands (離島). Tool returned Okinawa prefecture spots dominated by Naha-area gusuku castles (Nakagusuku, Tamaudun) — zero dark-sky or remote island stargazing content. Kumejima appears with nagisa_100 kind but no dark_sky / stargazing tag. The intent (離島 星空) is almost entirely unaddressed. Per RULE B retrieval failure is the single worst dim: the dark_sky/stargazing kind data for Okinawa remote islands is completely absent.
missing_data: Dark sky spots on Iriomote, Yonaguni, Miyako, Kume, Taketomi islands. No dark_sky_park or star_watching kind tags in the returned data.
improvement_hint: Add dark_sky / star_watching kind tags to remote Okinawa island entries. When query contains 星空/stargazing intent, filter or boost dark_sky_100 / dark_sky kind tagged entities.

reasoning: Query is in Thai asking for rural onsen lodgings in Hokkaido. Tool returned get_hotels for Hokkaido: top results are Nihon Hito-yu wo Mamoru Kai onsen ryokan (confirmed, authoritative) in rural locations (Shirinai, Yakumo, Tokachidake, Asahidake) — this is an excellent match for rural onsen. Constraint gap: Thai language not satisfied (response in ja/en). Per RULE D, constraint_handling -1 from otherwise-deserved 4. Per RULE B, language is the single constraint failure. The rural onsen intent is well addressed with authoritative official data.
missing_data: Thai-language descriptions. Rural onsen coordinates (most are null). More rural Hokkaido onsen beyond the 4 Hito-yu-kai entries.
improvement_hint: Add Thai (th) translation pass for hotel descriptions. Populate coordinates for Hito-yu-kai entries from address geocoding.

reasoning: Query is in Vietnamese asking for islands in the Seto Inland Sea. Tool fetched Kagawa prefecture spots; top results are Ritsurin Garden and Manno Lake — mainland Kagawa landmarks, not islands. The intent is specifically 瀬戸内 島々 (Seto islands). No island entities from Naoshima, Shodoshima, Teshima, Oshima, Omishima (Ehime side) appear. Retrieval failure: the island kind is absent from Kagawa results. The Seto Inland Sea also spans Hiroshima, Ehime, Okayama prefectures — single-prefecture fetch misses most of the relevant geography.
missing_data: Naoshima (contemporary art island), Shodoshima, Teshima, Oshima, Omishima. Multi-prefecture Seto fan-out covering Hiroshima/Okayama/Ehime island entries.
improvement_hint: Detect 瀬戸内/Seto Inland Sea regional queries and fan out across Kagawa, Hiroshima, Okayama, Ehime filtering by island kind. Also enrich island entities with spot-level data.

reasoning: Query asks for indigo dyeing workshops in Tokushima. Tool returned get_local_specialty craft items for Tokushima: 阿波正藍しじら織 (Awa Hon-ai Shijira Weave, indigo textile — relevant), 大谷焼 (ceramics — tangential), 阿波和紙 (washi with indigo dyeing — partially relevant). The core miss: no actual workshop or venue information. The user wants to experience/visit workshops (体験), but the tool returns METI craft designations (product categories, not places). Per RULE C, real authoritative craft data returned but scope gap: workshop venues entirely absent. RULE A applies — wrong tool partially; note in hint not dims.
missing_data: Actual indigo dyeing workshop venues in Tokushima (e.g., Aizuya, 阿波藍染体験工房). The craft designations don't provide visit/experience information.
improvement_hint: Link METI craft designations to get_spots venue entries tagged with craft_workshop / taiken kind. When query contains 体験/workshop intent, surface venue-level data alongside designation records.

reasoning: Query is in German asking for Buddhist temples with shukubo accommodation on Mount Koya. Tool returned get_hotels for Wakayama; top results are confirmed Koyasan Shukubo Association members (安養院, 一乗院, 惠光院, 北室院, 熊谷寺) — exact match, authoritative, specific. The response directly addresses the Koyasan shukubo intent with real lodge names, phones, and descriptions. One caveat: German query not served (response in ja/en), but per RULE D this counts as -1 on constraint_handling only (4→4 stays since already accounting for it). All dims otherwise excellent.
missing_data: German-language descriptions. Coordinates for shukubo entries (all null). Website URLs missing for most entries.
improvement_hint: Add German (de) translation pass for hotel descriptions. Geocode shukubo addresses to populate coordinates field.

reasoning: Query asks for off-the-beaten-path samurai districts outside Kyoto. Tool used search_area(q='武家屋敷') returning 153 matches. Top results include Buke Yashiki (Shimane), a Tokyo gate, and critically Kakunodate samurai district (Akita, Q11428562) with a detailed description mentioning weeping cherry blossoms, Important Preservation District designation, high prominence — excellent specificity. The 'outside Kyoto' constraint is satisfied as no Kyoto entries appear top. The query's 'off-the-beaten-path' framing is editorial (per policy, server is neutral) but the data surfaces diverse non-major-city locations.
missing_data: Other notable samurai districts: Kitsuki (Oita), Chiran (Kagoshima), Hagi (Yamaguchi), Kanazawa Higashi Chaya district.
improvement_hint: Boost buke_yashiki + preservation_district kind combination for samurai district queries. Add jokamachi / preservation_district cluster entries for Kitsuki, Chiran, Hagi.

reasoning: Query in Indonesian asks for summer festivals in Akita prefecture. Tool returned get_festivals for Akita in Indonesian (id): 3 items, all bunka_intangible — Dainichido Bugaku, Kakunodate Festival, Tsuchizaki Shinmei Festival. Critical miss: Akita's most famous summer festival, 竿燈まつり (Kanto Festival, one of Japan's big three Tohoku summer festivals) is absent. Also Omagari National Fireworks Competition (大曲の花火) and Noshiro Tanabata Festival are absent. Indonesian translation is provided, which addresses language constraint well. Coverage failure: only 3 results, missing most famous Akita summer festivals.
missing_data: 竿燈まつり (Kanto Festival, August), 大曲の花火 (Omagari Fireworks, August), Noshiro Summer Festival. These are the defining Akita summer festivals.
improvement_hint: Ensure scraped_schema_event entries from Akita tourism association are included in festival results. The 3 bunka_intangible entries are not 'summer festivals' per se — Dainichido Bugaku is a New Year ritual.

reasoning: Query in Korean asks for small fishing port towns in the San'in region. Tool returned Shimane spots: top results are Lake Shinji (lake), Matsue Castle (castle city), Iwami Ginzan (silver mine), Mount Sanbe (mountain) — no fishing port or coastal town entries. San'in region also includes Tottori prefecture which is absent. The fishing village / port town intent (漁港 町) is entirely unaddressed. Retrieval failure: no fishing_harbor / gyoko kind or coastal town entries for San'in.
missing_data: Fishing towns in San'in: Sakaiminato (Tottori/Shimane border, crab port), Masuda, Hamada, Oda fishing ports in Shimane. Tottori coast fishing villages. No 漁港/gyoko kind entries returned.
improvement_hint: Add fishing_harbor / coastal_town kind tags to San'in coastal settlements. Fan out across Shimane + Tottori for 山陰 regional queries.

reasoning: Query asks where to experience washi paper making in Gifu. Tool returned get_local_specialty craft for Gifu with 8 items including Mino Ware, Hida Shunkei, Nagiso Lathe Work, Ichii Ittobori — but 美濃和紙 (Mino Washi, METI traditional craft, Gifu's famous paper) is not visibly returned in the truncated 4000-char response. The tool returns craft designations not workshop venues. Similar to L2-14, user wants an 'experience' (体験) but gets product-level designation data. Per RULE C: authoritative craft data returned but the key item (Mino Washi) appears absent from truncated output and experience-level venue info is missing.
missing_data: 美濃和紙 (Mino Washi) METI craft designation. Workshop/experience venues: Mino Washi no Sato, Udatsu Townscape washi workshops in Mino City.
improvement_hint: Ensure 美濃和紙 appears in Gifu craft results (check if METI record is properly associated with prefecture code 21). Surface experience venue links alongside craft designations for 体験 queries.

reasoning: Query in Spanish asks for fishing villages on the Noto Peninsula. Tool fetched Ishikawa prefecture spots; top results are Kanazawa Castle and Kenroku-en garden — Kanazawa city landmarks, not Noto Peninsula fishing villages. The Noto Peninsula is a distinct sub-region requiring municipality-level filtering. No fishing village or port entries appear. Retrieval failure: prefecture-level get_spots without municipality filter returns Kanazawa-dominated results, completely missing Noto towns like Wajima, Suzu, Noto-cho coastal settlements.
missing_data: Noto Peninsula fishing villages: Wajima (輪島, lacquerware + morning market + fishing port), Suzu coast, Mitsuke-jima, Sosogi Coast, Noto-cho coastal hamlets.
improvement_hint: For sub-regional queries (Noto Peninsula, Izu Peninsula etc.), detect the toponym and use municipality-level filtering. Add fishing_harbor / gyoko / coastal_settlement kind tags to Noto coastal entries.

reasoning: Query asks for lesser-known sand dunes in Japan beyond Tottori. Tool used search_area(q='砂丘') returning 91 matches. Results include Tottori Sand Dunes (expected — present but not the focus), Onjuku Beach (Chiba), Ishikari local museum (Hokkaido, sand dune kind), Fukiagehama (Kagoshima, sand_dune + nagisa_100), Tsujido Beach (Kanagawa). Despite Tottori appearing at the top (expected given prominence), other prefectures are represented, satisfying the 'not just Tottori' intent. Authoritative data with QIDs. Minor: the Tottori duplicate entries (BUNKA-2127 and Q780905) add noise.
missing_data: Sarfuki Sand Dunes (Hokkaido), Nakatajima Sand Dunes (Shizuoka, Japan's three great sand dunes), Uchibo beach dunes.
improvement_hint: Deduplicate Tottori entries (BUNKA-2127 and Q780905 refer to same location). Boost non-Tottori sand_dune kind results when query explicitly requests alternatives.

reasoning: Query in Chinese asks for traditional crafts in the San'in region (山陰, Shimane + Tottori). Tool returned get_local_specialty for Shimane only, in Chinese. 4 items: Iwami Ware (ceramics), Sekishu Washi (paper), Unshuu Soroban (abacus), Izumo stone lanterns — all Shimane, legitimate METI crafts with excellent Chinese translations. Coverage gap: Tottori prefecture (part of San'in) is entirely absent. Tottori crafts (因州和紙, 弓浜絣) are missing. Per RULE C, authoritative Shimane data but scope gap (half of San'in region).
missing_data: Tottori prefecture crafts: 因州和紙 (Inshu Washi, METI), 弓浜絣 (Yumihama Gasuri, METI). These are the other half of San'in region.
improvement_hint: For 山陰 regional queries, automatically fan out to both Shimane (32) and Tottori (31) prefecture codes in get_local_specialty.

reasoning: Query asks for lavender fields in Hokkaido beyond Furano. Tool returned get_spots for Hokkaido: top results are Showa-Shinzan (volcano), Sapporo Clock Tower, Matsumae Castle — zero lavender field content. No lavender / lavender_farm kind tag entries appear. The intent (ラベンダー畑) is entirely unaddressed. Retrieval failure: lavender kind data is absent from the Hokkaido dataset. Furano's Farm Tomita and alternatives like Biei lavender fields are not present.
missing_data: Hokkaido lavender spots beyond Furano: Biei (青い池周辺ラベンダー), Kamifurano lavender farms, Nakafurano lavender fields. Even Furano Farm Tomita is absent.
improvement_hint: Add lavender_farm / lavender_field kind tags to Hokkaido flower farm entries. Enrich Furano/Biei area data with seasonal flower farm spot records.

reasoning: Query in Tagalog/Filipino asks for traditional old towns (古い町並み) in Kyushu. Tool fetched Oita spots; top results are Usuki Castle, Usa Shrine, Oka Castle — notable Oita spots but not 'traditional town streetscapes' (古い町並み). The query intent is historic preserved townscapes (preservation_district / jokamachi), not castles. Usuki does have a preserved townscape district, but it's not surfaced. Kyushu spans 7 prefectures; only Oita returned. Reasoning failure: data present but synthesized wrong — the tool-to-intent match is plausible (Oita has Usuki and Hita historical towns) but the ranking puts castles above preservation districts.
missing_data: Kyushu preservation districts: Usuki historic townscape, Hita Mameda-machi (Oita), Yanagawa (Fukuoka), Yame (Fukuoka), Chiran samurai district (Kagoshima). Multi-prefecture Kyushu fan-out.
improvement_hint: For 古い町並み / historic townscape intent, filter by preservation_district + jokamachi + kominka kind tags and boost preservation_district results. Fan out across all Kyushu prefectures.

reasoning: Query in Japanese asks for kominka (old folk houses) to stay in across Hokuriku (北陸, Ishikawa + Toyama + Fukui + Niigata). Tool returned get_hotels for Ishikawa only: results are onsen ryokan (Hito-yu-kai), business hotels (AB Hotel, ANA Crown Plaza), and a guest_house (かなや). No kominka lodging entries appear. 北陸 regional scope is also not met (only Ishikawa). Retrieval failure: kominka/machiya lodging type is absent from the hotel dataset.
missing_data: Kominka stay properties in Hokuriku: Gassho-style farmhouses in Shirakawa-go (Gifu border area), kominka accommodation in Kanazawa, Noto Peninsula farmhouses. Toyama, Fukui, Niigata kominka lodgings.
improvement_hint: Add kominka / machiya lodging_type entries to the hotel dataset. For 北陸 regional queries, fan out across Ishikawa, Toyama, Fukui (and optionally Niigata) prefecture codes.

reasoning: Query asks for mountain shrines on the Kii Peninsula. Tool used get_japan_heritage for Wakayama; top canonical heritage entries include Kongobu-ji (Mount Koya head temple), Kumano Kodo (UNESCO pilgrimage route), Kumano Sanzan (three grand shrines) — all directly relevant to mountain shrine / mountain faith on the Kii Peninsula. The tool_scope_note correctly flags that pilgrimage routes should use search_area/get_traditional_arts, but the wikidata_heritage entries returned are highly authoritative and specific. The Kumano Sanzan and Kodo are exactly what the query seeks.
missing_data: Nachi Taisha (Kumano Nachi Grand Shrine), Hongu Taisha, Hayatama Taisha as individual entries. Yoshino-Omine pilgrimage (Nara side). Mie prefecture mountain shrines (partial Kii Peninsula).
improvement_hint: Surface individual Kumano Sanzan shrine entries (Hongu, Nachi, Hayatama) as separate canonical heritage items rather than only the collective Kumano Sanzan entry.

reasoning: Query asks for summer fireworks festivals along the Sea of Japan coast. Tool returned get_festivals for Niigata with 129 items; 長岡まつり大花火大会 (Nagaoka Grand Fireworks, one of Japan's top 3, August 2-3) appears as the third result — a strong match. However: (1) top results are non-fireworks spring/flower festivals (芝桜まつり, ヒメサユリ祭り) which are not summer fireworks; (2) query scope is 'Sea of Japan coast' which spans Niigata, Akita, Yamagata, Ishikawa, Toyama, Fukui — only Niigata returned; (3) no ranking/filtering by fireworks kind. Coverage gap: no Akita Omagari or Yamagata coast fireworks.
missing_data: Akita 大曲の花火 (Omagari, Japan's #1 fireworks competition). Yamagata, Toyama, Fukui Sea of Japan coast fireworks. Better ranking to put fireworks events first in Niigata results.
improvement_hint: When query intent is hanabi/fireworks, filter or rank by hanabi / fireworks event_type. For Sea of Japan coast queries, fan out across relevant prefectures on the Japan Sea side.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional bamboo crafts in Oita. Tool returned get_local_specialty for Oita crafts: 別府竹細工 (Beppu Bamboo Crafts, METI designated, industry_category 木工品・竹工品) — exact match, authoritative, well described with production area (Beppu, Hita, Oita city etc.). Hakata Ori also appears (cross-prefecture textile, less directly relevant). The core answer is excellent: Beppu Bamboo Crafts is exactly what the query seeks, with rich description and official source URL.
missing_data: Visit/experience information: specific bamboo craft studios or exhibition halls in Beppu City. The museum venue (see_also_wikidata_venues) is partially helpful.
improvement_hint: Link Beppu Bamboo Crafts METI entry to get_spots venue entries tagged with bamboo_craft kind so agents can surface visiting locations alongside the designation.

reasoning: Query asks for cycling routes through rural Shikoku. Tool returned Ehime spots. Ōmishima (大三島) appears with cycling_route kind tag — a direct match for the Shimanami Kaido cycling route. However: (1) top results are castles (Ozu, Yuzuki, Uwajima) not cycling routes; (2) only Ehime returned, not all 4 Shikoku prefectures; (3) only one cycling_route tagged entity visible. Per RULE C, authoritative data but coverage gap (G): cycling routes across Kochi, Tokushima, Kagawa are absent, and Ehime results are dominated by non-cycling content.
missing_data: Cycling routes: Shimanami Kaido full route (spans Ehime+Hiroshima), Shikoku Karst Cycling Route (Kochi), Yoshino River cycling (Tokushima), Sanuki Cycle Route (Kagawa). Multi-prefecture Shikoku fan-out.
improvement_hint: For cycling queries, filter by cycling_route / cycling kind tag across all Shikoku prefectures. Add dedicated cycling_route entries for Shimanami Kaido and Shikoku inland routes.

reasoning: Query asks where to see traditional kabuki outside Tokyo and Kyoto. Tool used get_traditional_arts(lang='en') returning 183 items; Kabuki (UNESCO ICH) appears as the second item but describes kabuki as a form of dance-drama with no venue/location information. The top item is Bunraku (puppet theater, not kabuki). Subsequent items are unrelated performing arts (Chakkirako, Dainichido Bugaku). The response has the right art form present (kabuki at rank 2) but provides zero location data — no mention of rural kabuki venues like Iya Valley (Tokushima), Ogawa Kabuki (Nagano), or Chichibu Yomatsuri. Ranking failure: kabuki appears but venue/location data is completely absent.
missing_data: Regional kabuki venues: 人形浄瑠璃 venues in Tokushima (Awa Ningyo Joruri), Nagano Ogawa kabuki stage, Chichibu Night Festival kabuki, Kawakami Gogaku kabuki stage (Nagano). Venue-level get_spots data linked to kabuki kind.
improvement_hint: Link UNESCO kabuki entry to get_spots venue entries tagged with kabuki_theater / kabuki kind. Surface location-specific kabuki performance venues when kabuki intent is detected.
